domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides practical home activities focused on movement and physical skills for Noah, who is 10 months old and has Down syndrome. The activities are designed to support his current developmental level while encouraging growth in posture, strength, balance, and coordination.
We do not think Noah has a meaningful delay in Social / Emotional and may not need special support in this category right now.
This activity bank provides practical language and communication activities for Noah, focusing on sound play, turn-taking, and early word use, suitable for his developmental level.

This activity bank provides engaging and practical activities for Noah to support cognitive and adaptive skill development. The activities are tailored to his current abilities and near next steps, focusing on attention, problem-solving, and functional play.

activities.title: Tummy Time with Toys
activities.description: Place Noah on his tummy on a soft surface. Place colorful toys just out of reach to encourage him to push up onto his elbows or forearms to reach for them.

activities.title: Sound Imitation Game
activities.description: Make different sounds like 'ma', 'ba', and 'ah' and encourage Noah to imitate you. Use exaggerated facial expressions to engage him.

activities.title: Supported Sitting Practice
activities.description: Sit Noah on the floor with pillows around him for support. Encourage him to lean on his hands for balance while you place toys in front of him.

activities.title: Gesture Play
activities.description: Use simple gestures like waving or clapping and say the words associated with them. Encourage Noah to mimic the gestures while saying the words.

activities.title: Hand-to-Mouth Play
activities.description: Give Noah soft toys or safe objects to hold and encourage him to bring them to his mouth. This helps strengthen his grasp and coordination.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Language / Communication, Cognitive / Adaptive for Noah. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: We do not think Maya has a meaningful delay in Movement / Physical and may not need special support in this category right now.
We do not think Maya has a meaningful delay in Social / Emotional and may not need special support in this category right now.

This activity bank provides a variety of short, practical activities to support Maya's language and communication skills at home. The activities focus on encouraging vocalization, following directions, and using gestures to communicate.
We do not think Maya has a meaningful delay in Cognitive / Adaptive and may not need special support in this category right now.

activities.title: Name That Toy
activities.description: Hold up a familiar toy and say its name clearly. Encourage Maya to look at the toy when you name it. You can also ask her to point to the toy when you say its name.

activities.title: Follow the Direction
activities.description: Give Maya a simple one-step direction like 'give me the ball' without using gestures. Encourage her to respond by handing you the toy.

activities.title: Sound Imitation Game
activities.description: Make simple sounds (like animal noises) and encourage Maya to imitate them. Use animals she knows, like a dog (bark) or cat (meow).

activities.title: Point and Ask
activities.description: Place a few toys out of reach. Encourage Maya to point at the toy she wants and say a sound or word. Respond to her request by giving her the toy.

activities.title: Story Time with Sounds
activities.description: Read a short picture book with animals or objects. Encourage Maya to make sounds or point to pictures as you read. Pause to let her respond.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Language / Communication for Maya. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

concerns_bullets: Limited eye contact and few words

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides practical movement and physical activities for Leo, focusing on developing his physical skills while being mindful of his current abilities and next steps.
This activity bank provides practical home activities to support Leo's social and emotional development, focusing on engagement, interaction, and emotional connection.
This activity bank provides practical home activities to support Leo's language and communication skills, focusing on enhancing his ability to express himself and understand others.

This activity bank includes engaging and practical activities designed to support Leo's cognitive and adaptive skills at home. Each activity focuses on building attention, problem-solving, and functional play.

activities.title: Wave Goodbye
activities.description: Practice waving goodbye at different times, like when leaving a room or ending a play session. Encourage Leo to wave back.

activities.title: Excited Clapping
activities.description: Play a fun song and encourage Leo to clap along with you when he feels excited. You can model the clapping and show facial expressions of joy.

activities.title: Sound Imitation Game
activities.description: Make different animal sounds and encourage Leo to imitate them. Start with simple sounds like 'moo' or 'meow'.

activities.title: Hugging Time
activities.description: Select a stuffed toy and encourage Leo to hug it. Then, model hugging him and encourage him to hug you back, promoting affection and connection.

activities.title: Point and Ask
activities.description: Place a few toys in front of Leo. Encourage him to point to a toy he wants and say its name or make a sound.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Leo. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides practical movement and physical activities for Sofia to support her development in gross and fine motor skills.
We do not think Sofia has a meaningful delay in Social / Emotional and may not need special support in this category right now.
This activity bank provides practical home activities to support Sofia's language and communication skills, focusing on her current abilities and encouraging her development towards next milestones.
This activity bank provides a variety of cognitive and adaptive skill-building activities for Sofia, focusing on her current developmental level while also encouraging growth towards next milestones.

activities.title: Spoon Practice
activities.description: Give Sofia a bowl with some soft food (like yogurt or mashed bananas) and a spoon. Encourage her to scoop and eat independently, offering help as needed.

activities.title: Color Sorting
activities.description: Gather a few colored items (like blocks or crayons). Ask Sofia to sort them by color, saying 'Can you put all the red ones here?' and 'Now, can you find the blue ones?'

activities.title: Stair Climbing
activities.description: Supervise Sofia as she walks up and down a few stairs. Encourage her to hold onto the railing or your hand for support, focusing on her balance and strength.

activities.title: Two-Step Instructions
activities.description: Give Sofia two-step instructions like 'Pick up the toy and put it in the box.' Praise her when she follows through.

activities.title: Ball Kicking
activities.description: Set up a soft ball in a safe space. Encourage Sofia to kick the ball towards a target, like a box or a wall. Celebrate her attempts and encourage her to try again.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Language / Communication, Cognitive / Adaptive for Sofia. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides practical movement and physical activities for Ethan, focusing on improving his physical skills in a home setting.
We do not think Ethan has a meaningful delay in Social / Emotional and may not need special support in this category right now.
We do not think Ethan has a meaningful delay in Language / Communication and may not need special support in this category right now.

This activity bank provides a variety of cognitive and adaptive activities for Ethan, focusing on skill-building in a fun and engaging way. Each activity is designed to be practical for home and fits Ethan's developmental level.

activities.title: Spoon Practice
activities.description: Provide Ethan with a bowl of soft food and a spoon. Encourage him to scoop and eat independently, offering guidance as needed.

activities.title: Color Hunt
activities.description: Go on a color hunt around the house. Ask Ethan to find objects of a specific color and name them as he finds them.

activities.title: Ball Kicking
activities.description: Set up a soft ball in a safe space. Encourage Ethan to kick the ball forward, using his walker for support if needed.

activities.title: Counting Fun
activities.description: Count objects around the house together, such as toys or snacks. Help Ethan count up to 10.

activities.title: Jumping Fun
activities.description: Create a safe space with a soft surface. Encourage Ethan to jump with both feet, using a small trampoline or jumping in place.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Cognitive / Adaptive for Ethan. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides practical movement and physical activities for Amina, focusing on enhancing her skills in coordination, balance, and fine motor use. Activities are designed to be engaging and suitable for her developmental level.
This activity bank provides engaging social and emotional activities for Amina, focusing on enhancing her social engagement and emotional regulation skills.
This activity bank provides practical home activities to support Amina's language and communication skills, focusing on her current abilities and next steps.
This activity bank provides practical home activities to support Amina's cognitive and adaptive skills development, focusing on attention, problem-solving, and routines.

activities.title: Color Sorting
activities.description: Gather a few colored items (like blocks or crayons). Ask Amina to sort them into groups by color. You can say, 'Can you put all the red ones here?'

activities.title: Playful Pretend
activities.description: Choose a character together (like a superhero or teacher) and take turns pretending to be that character. Use simple phrases and actions to act out scenarios.

activities.title: Two-Step Instructions
activities.description: Give Amina simple two-step instructions like 'Pick up the toy and put it in the box.' Praise her when she follows the directions.

activities.title: Emotion Charades
activities.description: Take turns making faces to show different emotions (happy, sad, surprised). The other person guesses the emotion and then switches roles.

activities.title: Problem-Solving with a Stool
activities.description: Place a toy on a high shelf and encourage Amina to use a small stool to reach it. Guide her by saying, 'Can you stand on the stool to get the toy?'

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Amina. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides practical movement and physical activities for Lucas, focusing on developing his posture, strength, balance, coordination, reaching, grasping, sitting, crawling, standing, and walking skills.
This activity bank provides engaging social and emotional activities for Lucas, focusing on enhancing his social engagement and emotional regulation skills.
This activity bank provides practical language and communication activities for Lucas, focusing on sound production, turn-taking, and social interaction.

This activity bank provides practical, home-based activities to support Lucas's cognitive and adaptive skills development. Each activity is designed to be engaging and suitable for his current abilities while encouraging growth towards next milestones.

activities.title: Sound Imitation Game
activities.description: Make simple sounds like 'ahh' or 'ooo' and encourage Lucas to imitate you. Use a happy tone and smile to engage him.

activities.title: Sitting Up with Support
activities.description: Place Lucas in a sitting position with pillows around him for support. Encourage him to reach for toys placed slightly out of reach to practice balance and sitting without support.

activities.title: Sound Exploration with Toys
activities.description: Use toys that make sounds (like rattles or musical toys). Shake or press them to make sounds and encourage Lucas to mimic or respond.

activities.title: Hand Transfer Game
activities.description: Give Lucas a small toy in one hand and encourage him to move it to the other hand. You can model this by doing it yourself first.

activities.title: Follow the Sound
activities.description: Make a sound (like clapping or a simple word) and encourage Lucas to turn his head towards you. Reward him with smiles when he responds.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Lucas. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: We do not think Emma has a meaningful delay in Movement / Physical and may not need special support in this category right now.
We do not think Emma has a meaningful delay in Social / Emotional and may not need special support in this category right now.
This activity bank provides practical home activities to support Emma's language and communication skills, focusing on her current level and near next steps.
We do not think Emma has a meaningful delay in Cognitive / Adaptive and may not need special support in this category right now.

activities.title: Body Part Pointing
activities.description: Ask Emma to point to different body parts on herself and on a doll or stuffed animal. Start with familiar parts like 'nose' and 'eyes'.

activities.title: Two-Word Phrases
activities.description: Use toys or snacks to encourage Emma to say two-word phrases like 'more juice' or 'big truck'. Model the phrases and wait for her to imitate.

activities.title: Gesture Game
activities.description: Play a game where you and Emma take turns using gestures to communicate. For example, blowing a kiss or nodding yes. Encourage her to imitate your gestures.

activities.title: Book Exploration
activities.description: Choose a picture book and ask Emma to point to specific items, like 'Where is the bear?' Encourage her to make sounds or say words related to the pictures.

activities.title: Naming Objects in Books
activities.description: Look at a book together and point to different objects, asking Emma 'What is this?' Encourage her to name the objects or make sounds related to them.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Language / Communication for Emma. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides a variety of movement and physical activities tailored for Zain, focusing on developing his physical skills while being engaging and practical for home use.
This activity bank provides practical home activities to support Zain's social and emotional development, focusing on engagement, emotional regulation, and social interaction.
We do not think Zain has a meaningful delay in Language / Communication and may not need special support in this category right now.

This activity bank includes engaging and practical activities designed to support Zain's cognitive and adaptive skills, focusing on attention, problem-solving, and routine-following. Each activity is tailored to fit his developmental level while being enjoyable and manageable at home.

activities.title: Clean-Up Time Together
activities.description: At a designated clean-up time, encourage Zain to help pick up toys. Use a timer for 5 minutes and make it a fun challenge to see how many toys can be picked up together.

activities.title: Jumping Fun
activities.description: Create a safe space and encourage Zain to jump off a low surface (like a step or a cushion) using both feet. Start with a small height and gradually increase as he gains confidence.

activities.title: Look at Me Game
activities.description: Take turns with Zain to show fun actions or silly faces. Encourage him to say 'look at me' when he does something fun. This promotes shared attention and social engagement.

activities.title: Page Turner
activities.description: Read a picture book together and encourage Zain to turn the pages one at a time. Use a book with thicker pages for easier handling.

activities.title: Emotion Charades
activities.description: Take turns acting out different emotions (happy, sad, surprised) and have Zain guess which emotion it is. This encourages emotional recognition and expression.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Cognitive / Adaptive for Zain. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides practical movement and physical activities for Isla, focusing on developing her posture, strength, balance, coordination, and fine motor skills.
This activity bank includes engaging activities designed to support Isla's social and emotional development, focusing on eye contact, shared attention, and emotional connection.
This activity bank provides practical home activities to support Isla's language and communication skills, focusing on her current level and near next steps.
This activity bank provides practical and engaging activities to support Isla's cognitive and adaptive skills at home, focusing on attention, problem-solving, and functional play.

activities.title: Body Part Pointing
activities.description: Ask Isla to point to specific body parts on herself or on a doll, like 'Where's your nose?' or 'Show me the doll's eyes.'

activities.title: Spoon Practice
activities.description: Provide Isla with a bowl of soft food and a spoon. Encourage her to scoop and eat the food using the spoon. Offer assistance as needed to help her practice holding the spoon correctly.

activities.title: Two-Word Phrases
activities.description: Encourage Isla to use two-word phrases by modeling them. For example, say 'More juice' or 'Big truck' and encourage her to repeat after you.

activities.title: Ball Kicking
activities.description: Set up a soft ball in an open space. Encourage Isla to kick the ball with her foot. You can demonstrate and then take turns kicking the ball back and forth.

activities.title: Gesture Game
activities.description: Play a game where you model different gestures like blowing a kiss, nodding, or waving. Encourage Isla to imitate these gestures.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Isla. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: We do not think Mateo has a meaningful delay in Movement / Physical and may not need special support in this category right now.
This activity bank provides practical, engaging activities to support Mateo's social and emotional development, focusing on eye contact, shared attention, and social interaction.
This activity bank provides practical and engaging language and communication activities for Mateo, focusing on enhancing his communication skills through play and interaction.
This activity bank provides practical, home-based activities to support Mateo's cognitive and adaptive skills development, focusing on play and imitation.

activities.title: Sound Imitation Game
activities.description: Make different animal sounds and encourage Mateo to imitate them. Start with simple sounds like 'moo' for a cow or 'meow' for a cat. Use gestures to encourage him to join in.

activities.title: Chore Imitation
activities.description: Show Mateo how to sweep the floor with a broom. Encourage him to copy you while you sweep together.

activities.title: Follow the Gesture
activities.description: Show Mateo a simple gesture like waving or pointing. Encourage him to copy you. Then, ask him to do the gesture when you say a word related to it, like 'bye-bye' for waving.

activities.title: Toy Car Push
activities.description: Sit on the floor with Mateo and take turns pushing a toy car back and forth. Encourage him to push the car towards you.

activities.title: Simple Direction Following
activities.description: Give Mateo simple directions using both words and gestures, like 'come here' or 'give me the ball'. Celebrate when he follows your direction.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Social / Emotional, Language / Communication, Cognitive / Adaptive for Mateo. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

concerns_bullets: parents have very limited time for activities

domain_rows.summary: We do not think Luna has a meaningful delay in Movement / Physical and may not need special support in this category right now.
This activity bank provides practical home activities to support Luna's social and emotional development, focusing on engagement and interaction with caregivers and peers.
This activity bank provides practical language and communication activities for Luna, focusing on enhancing her speech and understanding through engaging and interactive tasks.
This activity bank provides practical, short activities to support Luna's cognitive and adaptive skills, focusing on attention, problem-solving, and functional play.

activities.title: Body Part Pointing
activities.description: Ask Luna to point to different body parts on herself or on a doll. Start with common ones like 'nose' and 'eyes'.

activities.title: Clean Up Time
activities.description: At a designated time, say 'It's clean up time!' and encourage Luna to help pick up her toys. Use eye contact and praise her efforts.

activities.title: Two-Word Phrases
activities.description: Encourage Luna to use two-word phrases by modeling them. For example, say 'more juice' and prompt her to repeat it.

activities.title: Look at Me Game
activities.description: Take turns with Luna to show each other something fun you can do. Encourage her to say 'Look at me!' when it's her turn.

activities.title: Gesture Game
activities.description: Play a game where you model different gestures (like blowing a kiss or nodding) and encourage Luna to imitate you.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Social / Emotional, Language / Communication, Cognitive / Adaptive for Luna. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank includes practical movement and physical activities designed to support Ryan's development in coordination, fine motor skills, and overall physical abilities. Each activity is tailored to fit his current developmental level while also encouraging progression.
We do not think Ryan has a meaningful delay in Social / Emotional and may not need special support in this category right now.
We do not think Ryan has a meaningful delay in Language / Communication and may not need special support in this category right now.
We do not think Ryan has a meaningful delay in Cognitive / Adaptive and may not need special support in this category right now.

activities.title: Dress-Up Challenge
activities.description: Provide Ryan with loose clothing items like a jacket or pants. Encourage him to put them on by himself. Offer assistance as needed, but let him try independently first.

activities.title: Ball Toss
activities.description: Stand a few feet away from Ryan and toss a large ball to him. Encourage him to catch it. If he misses, encourage him to try again. This helps with hand-eye coordination.

activities.title: Bead Stringing
activities.description: Give Ryan large beads or macaroni and a string. Encourage him to string them together, helping him if he struggles. Focus on his grip and coordination.

activities.title: Crayon Drawing
activities.description: Provide Ryan with crayons and paper. Encourage him to hold the crayon between his fingers and thumb, not in a fist, while drawing. Offer prompts for different shapes or lines.

activities.title: Pouring Practice
activities.description: With adult supervision, provide Ryan with a small pitcher of water and a cup. Encourage him to pour the water into the cup, focusing on control and balance.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical for Ryan. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.
